prerequisites: An active Smaily account with access to the account Preferences and Integrations settings.
An API user created under Preferences > Integrations > API Passwords, which yields your account subdomain, API username, and API password.
Your Smaily account subdomain (api_subdomain), which forms the API host https://<subdomain>.sendsmaily.net/api/.
The API username (api_username) and API password (api_password) for the created API user. The password is shown only once at creation, so save it immediately.

authMethods.name: HTTP Basic authentication (API username and password)
authMethods.summary: Smaily authenticates every API request with HTTP Basic auth using the API username (api_username) and API password (api_password) created for an API user. Requests are sent to your account host https://<api_subdomain>.sendsmaily.net/api/ over HTTPS; unsecured HTTP requests fail. Here api_password is the secret credential, while api_subdomain and api_username are non-secret connection settings.

setupSteps.title: Create an API user in Smaily
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Smaily, select your account name in the upper right corner, and choose Preferences from the dropdown. Open the Integrations tab and, under API Passwords, click Create a new user. Smaily generates your account subdomain, API username, and API password. Copy the API password immediately because it is displayed only once.

setupSteps.title: Provide the subdomain, username, and password
setupSteps.body: Configure the connector with api_subdomain (your account subdomain, used to build the host https://<api_subdomain>.sendsmaily.net/api/), api_username (the generated API username), and api_password (the generated API password). All three fields are required, and api_password is stored as a secret.

setupSteps.title: Verify the connection
setupSteps.body: The connector authenticates over HTTPS using HTTP Basic auth against https://<api_subdomain>.sendsmaily.net/api/. Confirm the subdomain, username, and password are correct so requests authenticate successfully; mismatched or expired credentials cause authentication failures.

sources.title: Smaily Help: Create an API user
